schemes.source_text: National Food Security Mission was launched in 2007-08 to increase the production of rice, wheat and pulses. Later, coarse cereals, nutri cereals, commercial crops (sugarcane, jute, and cotton) were also added to the mission.

schemes.source_text: A subsidy to the extent of 75% of the cost for the purchase of an insulated box of at least 50 liters capacity. Only the fisherman engaged in selling and marketing fish, fishing at sea with a canoe, and fishing at sea with a mechanized fishing vessel with upto 6-cylinder engines are eligible.

schemes.name: Anti Bird Net
schemes.source_text: Under the Anti Bird Net Birds will never leave your garden if they are used to pecking on certain trees and plants, but if you install anti-bird nets at the appropriate places, you can prevent them from coming again to peck your plants.

schemes.source_text: The scheme provides financial assistance to dairy farmers belonging to General Caste category on purchase of electric powered chaff cutters. schemes.name: National Agriculture Market
schemes.source_text: Launched on 14th April 2016, e-NAM is a pan-India electronic trading platform that links existing mandis to create a unified online market for agricultural commodities, promoting “One Nation One Market” and enabling transparent, efficient, and nationwide agri-trade.
schemes.description: Launched on 14th April 2016, e-NAM is a pan-India electronic trading platform that links existing mandis to create a unified online market for agricultural commodities, promoting “One Nation One Market” and enabling transparent, efficient, and nationwide agri-trade.
schemes.benefit: e-NAM is a pan-India electronic trading platform that links existing mandis to create a unified online market for agricultural commodities, promoting “One Nation One Market” and enabling transparent, efficient, and nationwide agri-trade.

schemes.name: Krishonnati Yojana - Sub Mission On Seed And Planting Material (SMSP)
schemes.source_text: The Sub-Mission on Seeds and Planting Material (SMSP) aims to increase the production of certified / quality seed, increase SRR, upgrade the quality of farm-saved seeds, strengthen the seed multiplication chain, promote new technologies and methodologies in seed production, processing, testing, etc.

schemes.name: Chief Minister Samagra Gramya Unnayan Yojana
schemes.source_text: A 5-year mega-mission called Chief Minister Samagra Gramya Unnayan Yojana (CMSGUY) was launched in the Financial Year(FY) 2016-17, which is scheduled to culminate in the FY 2021-22, coinciding with 75 years of India's Independence.

schemes.name: Krushi Mahotsav
schemes.source_text: The Krishi Mahotsav Yojana is an agricultural development scheme launched by the Government of Gujarat in the year 2010. The scheme aims to boost agricultural productivity and increase the income of farmers in the state.
schemes.description: The Krishi Mahotsav Yojana is an agricultural development scheme launched by the Government of Gujarat in the year 2010. The scheme aims to boost agricultural productivity and increase the income of farmers in the state.

schemes.name: ₹ 5 Lakh Insurance Cover To Farmers
schemes.source_text: Launched on 15 August 2018, the scheme "₹5 Lakh Insurance Cover for Farmers" is an Insurance Scheme by the Department of Agriculture, Government of Telangana. This scheme provides ₹5 lakh insurance coverage to all farmers aged between 18 and 60 years.

schemes.name: Atma Nirbhar Krishi Yojana
schemes.source_text: Atma Nirbhar Krishi Yojana (ANKY) is a scheme launched by the Arunachal Pradesh government to boost the agriculture sector. It provides financial assistance to farmers for various agricultural activities

schemes.name: Arun Pig Development Scheme
schemes.source_text: The scheme “Arun Pig Development Scheme” was launched by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Veterinary & Dairy Development, Government of Arunachal Pradesh in the financial year 2019-20 with a focus on pig rearing.

schemes.name: Nationwide Artificial Insemination Programme
schemes.source_text: The Nationwide Artificial Insemination Programme (NAIP), is a flagship program of the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying. The objective of the programme is to increase the productivity of livestock by improving the genetic potential through artificial insemination.

schemes.name: Cluster Nutritional Kitchen Garden Scheme
schemes.source_text: The Cluster Nutritional Kitchen Garden Scheme (CNKGS) is an initiative launched by the government of Arunachal Pradesh to promote self-sufficiency and improve nutritional needs by encouraging the establishment of kitchen gardens.

schemes.source_text: The scheme “Subsidy Scheme for Establishment of Goat Unit (10+1) for Scheduled Tribe Women” was launched by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat to uplift Economically poor livestock breeders of Gujarat State.

Birds Unit for Divyang People” was launched by the Dept. of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Govt. of Gujarat to provide employment, nutritious diet and to raise the income of Divyang People of the society of Gujarat State.

schemes.source_text: The scheme strives to back the farmers with financial incentives in the event of a crop loss due to natural calamities.

schemes.name: Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Water Augmentation
schemes.source_text: The scheme "Development Support for Coffee in Traditional Areas: Water Augmentation" by the Coffee Board, Department of Commerce, aims to improve the production and productivity of coffee plantations by encouraging water augmentation in traditional areas.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Construction/ Purchase of Wooden/ FRP Fishing Vessel (Canoe) for Fishermen
schemes.source_text: Through this scheme, 50% of the actual cost of the Wooden/ Fiber-Reinforced Plastic (FRP) Fishing Vessel (Canoe) purchased by the fishermen is covered by the Government of Goa. The applicant will be entitled to a subsidy grant once every four years on one craft per family household.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance to Brackish Water Aquaculture Farms
schemes.source_text: Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to eligible fishermen for the construction and renovation of the Farm, for the purchase of farming equipment, and for the purchase of feed. Only the farmers having a water spread area of 2 hectares or less can apply to this scheme.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Setting up Crab Culture Unit
schemes.source_text: Through this scheme, financial assistance of 25% of the actual cost, limited to ₹1,50,000 per hectare is provided to eligible fishermen for setting up crab culture unit. Only Fish Farmer/ Individual/ Registered Self Help Group/ Societies, interested in the setting up of the Crab Farming Unit.

schemes.source_text: "Grant of 50% Subsidy for the Purchase of Aquarium and Its Accessories" is introduced with the objective to propagate the importance of aquarium keeping and to inculcate the art of aquarium keeping among the general public. schemes.name: Coconut Palm Insurance Scheme
schemes.source_text: The “Coconut Palm Insurance Scheme (CPIS)” is being implemented by the Coconut Development Board, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India with the objective of insuring coconut palms against natural calamities, climatic risks, pests, diseases, and other perils.

schemes.name: "Annual Insurance Premium for Boat Owners" under "Financial Assistance for Availing Subsidy Components for the Development of Marine Fisheries"
schemes.source_text: "Annual Insurance Premium for Boat Owners" under "Financial Assistance for Availing Subsidy Components for the Development of Marine Fisheries" is introduced to increase marine fish production through subsidies for the annual premiums for insurance coverage paid by the mechanized boat operators.

schemes.source_text: The scheme "Conversion/Modernisation of Mechanised Fishing Boats for Deep Sea Fishing" is introduced by the Dept of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Govt of Puducherry to increase marine fish production by extending grants for the conversion/modernization of mechanized boats for deep-sea fishing.

schemes.source_text: Through the scheme “Replanting & Rejuvenation of Coconut Garden (R&R)" by the Coconut Development Board, the coconut farmers are provided financial assistance through DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) mode for the cutting & removal, replanting, and for integrated management practices.

schemes.source_text: The objective of the scheme is to make available to the farmers good quality planting materials in convergence mode and sale at subsidized rates.

schemes.source_text: The objectives of the scheme are to boost up the area and production of all plantation crops like Arecanut, Cashewnut, Coconut, Pineapple etc., and to provide a 50 % subsidy for planting materials and construction of a soakage tank.

schemes.source_text: The objective of the scheme is to motivate the farmers to take up floriculture (traditional and non-traditional flowers) as a commercial venture through the distribution of planting materials and other inputs.

schemes.name: Assistance to General Farmers for Installation of Submersible / Centrifugal Motor Pumpsets With Accessories
schemes.source_text: Through this scheme, the farmers from the General Category are provided with a grant of 65% subsidy towards the installation of suitable Submersible / Centrifugal motor pump sets with accessories, subject to a maximum of ₹50,000/‐ per tubewell per individual.

schemes.name: Plant Protection including Integrated Pest Management Scheme
schemes.source_text: The objective of the scheme is to mobilize and support farmers to use bio-pesticides and bio- agents for plant health management through distribution of bio-pesticides and other IPM tools and through sale of Sprayers and Plant Protection Equipment to encourage organic crop production.

schemes.name: Farm Pond (Khet Talai) Nirman Karyakram
schemes.source_text: Under the Farm Pond (Khet Talaee) Nirmaan Kaaryakram scheme provied to help the farmers of Rajasthan to collect rain water and use it for irrigation purposes so that the farmers can get sufficient water for irrigation in their fields.

schemes.name: Diggy
schemes.source_text: Rajasthan Government has started the Diggy Scheme for the welfare of farmers, under which farmers will be provided grants for making dig for irrigation of crops. This scheme is to help farmers improve their farming by using more water resources and encourage them to grow good crops.

schemes.name: Winter Cropping & Development of Cultivable Land Scheme
schemes.source_text: The objectives of the scheme are to bring more areas under cultivation during the normally lean winter season under different crops other than paddy and increase production and productivity of various crops like cereals, pulses, oilseeds, and vegetables during the Rabi season.

schemes.name: Mini Sprinkler Sanyantr
schemes.source_text: The Mini Sprinkler Sanyantr scheme provided a subsidy to the farmers can irrigate more land by getting sprinkler subsidy. The subsidy given by the state government to the farmers will be sent directly to their bank account through DBT.

schemes.name: Green House
schemes.source_text: Under the Green House Scheme Farmers can get the benefit of subsidy on green house and earn more income by cultivating horticultural crops like vegetables, flowers, fruits etc. by controlling the ago-climatic factors, temperature, humidity and sunlight.

schemes.source_text: Under the Kam Lagat Pyaaj Bhandaran Sanrachna yojana Construction of warehouses should be done at a high level to prevent moisture and dampness which occur due to direct contact of onions with soil.

schemes.source_text: Under this scheme, unemployed persons will be assisted by way of providing financial assistance in form of subsidy for establishing dairy units of 4, 10, 20 & 50 milch animals like buffaloes, indigenous cows (Hariana, Sahiwal, Belahi, Red Sindhi, Tharparkar and Gir breeds only) and crossbred cows.

schemes.source_text: This is a credit-linked scheme with a primary objective of providing self-employment opportunities to Schedule castes, the unemployed persons will be assisted by way of establishing Dairy units, Piggery units, and Sheep/Goat units.

schemes.name: "Protected Cultivation" Component of "Establishment of New Gardens (Area Expansion)" Scheme
schemes.source_text: Through this scheme, the farmers are provided Poly House, Shade Net House (Tubular Structure), Plastic Mulching at subsidised rates for practising Protected Cultivation. In Puducherry, this scheme is implemented by the Department of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, Union Territory of Puducherry.

schemes.name: Tuber Crops Potato, Tapioca, Colocasia
schemes.source_text: The objective of the scheme is to boost up production and productivity of potato per unit area by providing quality HYV seeds, and equipment at a 50% subsidy.

schemes.name: Distribution of Vegetable Seeds Minikit & Fruits Minikit at Free of Cost
schemes.source_text: “Distribution of Vegetable Seeds Minikit & Fruits Minikit at Free of Cost" was launched by the Dept of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, UT of Puducherry. All the horticulture growers of rural & urban areas are provided with vegetable seeds minikit & fruits minikit worth ₹250/‐ free of cost.

schemes.name: "Sugarcane Nursery Premium" Sub-Scheme of the "Crop Production Technology" Scheme
schemes.source_text: The "Sugarcane Nursery Premium" Sub-Scheme of the "Crop Production Technology" Scheme is introduced by the Dept of Agriculture and Farmer's Welfare, UT of Puducherry. Through this scheme, the farmers are provided Nursery Premium for Raising Seed Material to Plant One Hectare of Sugarcane.

schemes.name: Bhausaheb Fundkar Falbag Lagvad Scheme
schemes.source_text: This scheme includes, Plantation of sixteen perennial horticulture crops. Farmer from Konkan division can avail benefit of 0.10 Hector  to 10.00 Hector  and rest Of Maharashtra 0.20 Hector  to 6.00 Hector

schemes.name: Krshak Prashikshan Tatha Bhraman Karyakram
schemes.source_text: Under the scheme, Horticulture and Food Processing Department provide visit to the different location to make the farmers aware of the new technologies of agronomy by giving them tours.

schemes.name: Nanaji Deshmukh Krishi Sanjivani Prakalp
schemes.source_text: The project aims to enhance climate-resilience and profitability of smallholder farming systems in 15 project districts of Maharashtra through promotion of climate resilient technology and commodity value chain across approximately 5,000 drought-prone and/or sodicity/salinity affected villages.

schemes.name: Ramie Crop Scheme
schemes.source_text: The objective of the scheme is to encourage cultivation of perennial and remunerative textile crop such as Ramie in suitable areas by providing necessary inputs and training.
schemes.description: The objective of the scheme is to encourage cultivation of perennial and remunerative textile crop such as Ramie in suitable areas by providing necessary inputs and training.

schemes.name: Agriculture Information Unit Scheme
schemes.source_text: The key objective is to disseminate information on Scientific methods of cultivation to extension staff and farming community through media such as pamphlets, leaflets, radio, television etc. and participating/holding awareness programs by means of exhibitions, seminars etc.
schemes.description: The key objective is to disseminate information on Scientific methods of cultivation to extension staff and farming community through media such as pamphlets, leaflets, radio, television etc. and participating/holding awareness programs by means of exhibitions, seminars etc.

schemes.name: State Rice Mission Scheme
schemes.source_text: The objective of the scheme is to increase productivity and production of rice through improved technologies and extension methods and, to supply the quality/certified seeds/organic fertilizers at 50% subsidy.

schemes.name: Jal Hauj
schemes.source_text: The objective of the Jal Hauj scheme is to promote sustainable water management practices and address water scarcity issues by facilitating the construction of water harvesting structures.

schemes.name: Plastic Tunnel (Lo-Tunnel)
schemes.source_text: Plastic tunnels (low tunnels) provide benefits to farmers. These are protected structures created by covering each crop bed with a low-lying plastic sheet after the crops have been planted in the main field.
schemes.description: Plastic tunnels (low tunnels) provide benefits to farmers. These are protected structures created by covering each crop bed with a low-lying plastic sheet after the crops have been planted in the main field.

schemes.name: Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme (ST Category)
schemes.source_text: The scheme “Fodder Minikits under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP) Scheme” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Government of Gujarat to provide an improved variety of fodder seeds to Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries for widespread publicity, demonstration, and adaptation by farmers.

schemes.name: Destitute Agricultural Labourers Pension Scheme
schemes.source_text: The "Destitute Agricultural Labourers Pension Scheme" was launched by the Social Welfare and Nutritious Meal Programme Department, Government of Tamil Nadu. The scheme aims to give pensions of ₹1,000/- to destitute agricultural labourers of 60 years and above and dhoti, saree supplied on festivals.

schemes.name: Scheme for Establishment of Hi-Tech and Mini Dairy Units under Mukhya Mantri Antyodaya Parivaar Utthan Yojana
schemes.source_text: Under this scheme, a resident of Haryana who is between 18 and 60 years of age can avail a 25% subsidy on the cost of two milch animals. The subsidy can be availed as a loan for the purchase of these milch animals by the beneficiary for the establishment of dairy units with two milch animals.

The subsidy can be availed as a loan for the purchase of these milch animals by the beneficiary for the establishment of dairy units with two milch animals.

schemes.name: Scheme for Establishment of Backyard Poultry Units under Mukhya Mantri Antyodaya Parivaar Utthan Yojana
schemes.source_text: Under this scheme, 50 chicks of 10 days of age from the Government Hatchery Farm along with two feeders and two water drinkers are provided free of cost to each beneficiary family at their doorstep.

schemes.name: Scheme for Providing Employment Opportunities by Establishing Piggery, Sheep and Goat Units (General)
schemes.source_text: This scheme is a credit linked scheme with a primary objective of providing self-employment opportunities, the unemployed persons will be assisted by way of establishing Sheep or Goat Units (15 female+1 male) and Piggery Unit (10 female+1 male).

schemes.name: Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry
schemes.source_text: The Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying introduced the National Livestock Mission as umbrella scheme. The "Sub-mission on Breed Development of Livestock and Poultry" is the realigned National Livestock Mission, which is the scheme's sub-mission.

schemes.name: Fasal Pradarshan
schemes.source_text: Under this scheme, the Agriculture Department organizes field and crop demonstrations to showcase advanced crop production methods and the latest technologies to farmers.

schemes.name: Distribution of Soil Health Card
schemes.source_text: Under the “Distribution of Soil Health Card” scheme, Soil health cards will be distributed to all the farmers who have offered the soil sample for testing by paying a prescribed fee of ₹5/- each for Macro and Micronutrient testing.

schemes.name: Micro Nutrient Spray
schemes.source_text: The “Micro Nutrient Spray” scheme provides a 50% subsidy or ₹70/- per hectare to eligible farmers, with a focus on ensuring benefits to SC/ST and women farmers.

schemes.name: District Central Cooperative Banks and through its Branches: For SSI Unit Loan and Other NFS Activities
schemes.source_text: This scheme provides financial assistance for SSI unit loans and other Non-Farm Sector (NFS) activities through District Central Cooperative Banks and their branches.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Aam Vikas Yojana
schemes.source_text: The Scheme aims to expand plantain cultivation across all districts in Bihar. Farmers can receive subsidies for up to 10 acres and access high-quality tissue-cultured plants for cultivation.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Strawberry Vikas Yojana
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to promote strawberry cultivation across 20 districts in Bihar. It provides subsidies on seedlings, packaging materials, and equipment to support farmers in enhancing strawberry production.

schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to increase the production of Kufri Chipsona variety suitable for potato processing in Bihar by expanding its area and to increase the income of farmers by promoting processing.

schemes.name: Supply of Kadaknath Chicks on Subsidy
schemes.source_text: The scheme "Supply of Kadaknath Chicken on Grant" aims to improve the economic condition of beneficiaries through poultry farming and protect and promote the Kadaknath breed. Beneficiaries receive 40 chicks along with food grains, medicine, and transportation support for 28 days.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Jan-Van Scheme
schemes.source_text: The Mukhyamantri Jan-Van Scheme aims to promote tree plantation on private lands in Jharkhand to enhance farmers' income, conserve the environment, and reduce pressure on notified forests. Farmers are encouraged to plant trees on their lands with incentives covering 75% of plantation and maintenance

schemes.source_text: The "Training on Artificial Insemination for Self-Employment" by the Animal Husbandry Department, Madhya Pradesh promotes milk production and employment. Trained Gausevaks get an honorarium of ₹4,000 and equipment worth ₹18,000.

schemes.source_text: The "Gopal Incentive Scheme" by the Animal Husbandry Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, promotes the rearing of Indian high-breed cattle and boosts milk production, benefiting animal breeders and livestock farmers with access to Indian advanced breed cows.

schemes.name: Millet Development Scheme
schemes.source_text: The "Millet Development Scheme" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Dept., Gujarat, aims to promote millet cultivation in Gujarat by organizing promotional activities, expos, processing units, demonstrations(seed/input kit ), and taluka-level melas.

schemes.name: Establishment of Goat Unit (10 +1)
schemes.source_text: The "Establishment of Goat Unit (10+1)" scheme by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to promote the social and economic upliftment of goat breeders by encouraging goat breeding and genetic improvement through pure breeding.

schemes.name: Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (General Category)
schemes.source_text: The scheme "Assistance of Concentrate Feed After Parturition (General Category)" provides 150 kg of BIS Type II concentrate feed to General Category livestock owners after parturition to support cattle health, increase milk production, and enhance calf development.

schemes.name: Mahila Bakri Palan
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to empower women, especially abandoned, widowed, destitute, single, and disaster-affected women, by providing them support for goat rearing to generate livelihood and improve household income.

schemes.source_text: This scheme provides financial assistance to farmers practicing natural farming in the completely chemical-free Dang district to promote sustainable agriculture, increase productivity, and ensure environmental conservation.

schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to provide 4% interest subvention to the fishermen in Gujarat on timely repayment of short-term loans taken through the Kisan Credit Card.

schemes.source_text: This scheme provides financial assistance for the maintenance of one indigenous cow to farmers who practice completely natural farming methods using native cows.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Rajya Krishi Vikas Yojana
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to help sugarcane farmers grow better crops, increase production, improve sugar quality, and provide support through high-quality seeds, modern techniques, mechanization, crop protection, and advanced farming practices.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Bhed/Bakri (Sheep/Goat) Palak Uthan Yojana for Scheduled Castes
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to uplift Scheduled Caste beneficiaries. Through this scheme, sheep and goat units are provided free of cost, along with veterinary assistance, to eligible unemployed Scheduled Caste families.

schemes.name: "Interest Subvention on Working Capital" under the "Assam Tea Industries Special Incentives Scheme"
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to ensure long term financial sustainability of the Tea industry and incentivize Orthodox tea production over Crush, Tear, Curl tea. It provides interest subvention, production subsidies, plant & machinery subsidies, tax holidays to tea plantations and manufacturing units in Assam.

schemes.name: PMMSY: Inputs for Saline /Alkaline Water Aquaculture - Haryana
schemes.source_text: The subsidy for Inputs for Saline /Alkaline Water Aquaculture under Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) provides input cost subsidy for saline/alkaline water aquaculture in newly created water bodies to expand fisheries production in Haryana.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-yojana (PM-MKSSY): COMPONENT 1B: One-Time Incentive for the Adoption of Aquaculture Insurance
schemes.source_text: The Component 1B of PM-MKSSY provides a one-time incentive to eligible applicants who have availed institutional credit for fisheries-related activities. The objective is to encourage formal credit adoption and support the repayment capacity of fisheries microenterprises.

schemes.name: Intensive Fisheries Development Programme - Subsidy on Solar System for fish/shrimp culture
schemes.source_text: Under this scheme, subsidies on solar systems for fish/shrimp culture are provided to eligible fish and shrimp farmers. The subsidy is given at 60% of the total project cost, or ₹30,000 per kW, whichever is lower. The maximum subsidy is limited to 30 kW for shrimp farmers and 10 kW for fish farmers.

schemes.name: PMMSY: Installation of Cages in Reservoirs Scheme - Haryana
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to promote cage fish farming. It provides grants per unit to the individuals and groups for sustainable aquaculture practices.
schemes.description: The scheme aims to promote cage fish farming. It provides grants per unit to the individuals and groups for sustainable aquaculture practices.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana: Establishment of Backyard mini RAS Units - Haryana
schemes.source_text: Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of subsidies is provided to eligible beneficiaries for establishing these units. Various surface and groundwater sources such as canals, rivers, springs, open wells, and tube wells can be utilized for operating RAS units.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Purchase of Barrier Net and its Accessories
schemes.source_text: “Financial Assistance for Purchase of Barrier Net and its Accessories” is a scheme by Dept. of Fisheries, Govt. of Goa. The scheme provides free insurance for livestock, enhancing the economic security of resource-poor SC families. The scheme covers milch animals, sheep, goats, pigs, camels, etc;

schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to protect the endangered Whale Shark, the world's largest fish, along the Gujarat coast, a crucial breeding site. schemes.name: Chief Minister Floriculture Mission
schemes.source_text: This is a special scheme initiated during 2014-15 aimed at all-round development of floriculture sector in the State which incorporates cultivation, marketing, training etc. For the year 2015- 16 cultivation of Gerbera under Shade net house with tissue cultured planting materials is proposed.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme on Agriculture Cooperation

schemes.name: Grant of Subsidy for Purchase of Mobile Sea Food Van
schemes.source_text: The scheme aims to provide the beneficiary with a mobile seafood van. schemes.source_text: GOBARdhan Scheme was launched in April 2018 as a part of the Solid and Liquid Waste Management component under Swachh Bharat Mission (Grameen) to positively impact village cleanliness and generate wealth and energy from cattle and organic waste. The main focus of GOBARDHAN is to keep villages clean.

schemes.name: Dr. Ambedkar Medhavi Chattar Sansodhit Yojna
schemes.source_text: This scheme is being implemented to encourage the merit amongst the students of Scheduled Castes, Backward Classes, Nomadic, DNTs, Semi-Nomadic, Vimukt Jaati and Tapriwas Jaati and to produce a feeling of competition amongst them and motivate them to earn more and more marks from the middle to post.
schemes.description: This scheme is being implemented to encourage the merit amongst the students of Scheduled Castes, Backward Classes, Nomadic, DNTs, Semi-Nomadic, Vimukt Jaati and Tapriwas Jaati and to produce a feeling of competition amongst them and motivate them to earn more and more marks from the middle to post.
schemes.benefit: The scheme aims to encourage merit amongst students and produce a feeling of competition amongst them to earn more and more marks.

schemes.name: Chikitsa Pratipoorti Yojana
schemes.source_text: "Chikitsa Pratipoorti Yojana" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial assistance to the Registered Workers for the treatment of serious illnesses (cancer, heart disease, kidney disease, etc.
schemes.description: "Chikitsa Pratipoorti Yojana" is a scheme by the Department of Labour, Employment, Training & Skill Development Govt of Jharkhand. The scheme provides financial assistance to the Registered Workers for the treatment of serious illnesses (cancer, heart disease, kidney disease, etc.
schemes.benefit: Financial assistance is provided to Registered Workers for the treatment of serious illnesses.

schemes.name: Aawaas Sahayata
schemes.source_text: Launched in 2016, the "Aawaas Sahayata" scheme by the Department of Tribal Affairs, Madhya Pradesh, The scheme provides housing assistance to tribal students pursuing higher education outside their hometowns where hostels are unavailable.
schemes.description: Launched in 2016, the "Aawaas Sahayata" scheme by the Department of Tribal Affairs, Madhya Pradesh, The scheme provides housing assistance to tribal students pursuing higher education outside their hometowns where hostels are unavailable.
schemes.benefit: The scheme provides housing assistance to tribal students pursuing higher education outside their hometowns where hostels are unavailable.

schemes.name: Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer
schemes.source_text: “Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Puducherry, was introduced to extend a helping hand by way of financial assistance to Old Age/Destitute Pensioners who are suffering from TB/Cancer to meet the medical expenses.
schemes.description: “Financial Assistance to Old Age Pensioner Suffering From TB/ Cancer” by the Dept of Women & Child Development, Govt of Puducherry, was introduced to extend a helping hand by way of financial assistance to Old Age/Destitute Pensioners who are suffering from TB/Cancer to meet the medical expenses.
schemes.benefit: Financial assistance to meet medical expenses for Old Age/Destitute Pensioners suffering from TB/Cancer.
